title: USB Themed Wireframes
content: Wireframes I built on top of the USB Design System to show how the variable architecture works in practice. Switching brands in Figma's collections dropdown updates colors, logos, and brand-specific styling across all screens at once — the system doing what it was set up to do. These ran across 4 projects and roughly 500 Figma frames.

media.caption: Dynamic Brand Theming: Selecting a brand from Figma's collections dropdown switches the wireframes between Acme, Fidelity, and Elan — updating colors, logos, and brand styling while the layout and components stay the same.

Wireframe Component Detail: A closer look at one branded wireframe — header, content cards with chevrons, link treatments, and footer, showing how the design system tokens apply at the component level.
System Scale: A Figma overview of the 4 major projects using the USB Design System — roughly 500 frames across approximately 10 project files.
Header Exploration Sketches: Hand-drawn sketches working through header and navigation ideas before moving into Figma. These informed the component structure in the final wireframes.

media.alt: Refined Header Design and Production Impact
media.caption: From Sketch to Production: The refined header design next to a screenshot from the US Bank online mobile banking page. Our team didn't ship this version, but the navigation patterns were picked up by another team at USB.
